sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Green Dot considers sale as CEO exits
feed.summary: Green Dot, an embedded finance company, is exploring "potential strategic alternatives," including a possible sale, after posting a $26.7 million loss last year. The company's CEO, George Gresham, has also exited his role and been replaced by interim CEO William Jacobs. This upheaval follows Green Dot grappling with shrinking profit margins and a $44 million fine from the Federal Reserve.

feed.title: Intuit QuickBooks Enables Tap to Pay on iPhone for Small and Mid-Market Businesses to Accept Contactless Payments
feed.summary: Intuit Inc. announced the availability of Tap to Pay on iPhone for its QuickBooks Online customers in the U.S., allowing small and mid-market businesses to accept contactless payments using just an iPhone, without additional hardware. This feature integrates directly with QuickBooks Online, streamlining financial management and accelerating cash flow for merchants. The company highlights that this solution addresses common challenges faced by small businesses, such as cash flow issues and the need for flexible payment options, while also offering lower processing fees and secure transactions.

feed.title: MAGS: 3 Reasons The Dive Is Probably Not Over (BATS:MAGS)
feed.summary: The article argues that the "Magnificent 7" stocks, represented by the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS), are facing continued trouble and a short-term bounce is unlikely to resolve underlying issues. The author attributes this to these stocks being overbought, the market's excessive focus on them, and the top-heavy nature of the U.S. market, increasing the risk of a swift downturn. The author prefers using QQQ calls for limited exposure to these dominant stocks.

feed.title: SanDisk to Hike NAND Prices by Over 10% as US Memory Giant Expects Supply to Tighten
feed.summary: SanDisk, a leading memory chip maker, plans to increase its NAND flash memory prices by over 10% from April 1st in anticipation of a supply shortage. This decision follows production cuts by major OEMs due to a previous surplus, and while it signals a potential market recovery, its acceptance by clients and the impact of weak demand in consumer electronics remain uncertain. The long-term outlook depends on the recovery of PC and smartphone demand and the adoption of AI features in gadgets.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation Poised to Gain From U.S. Tariffs & Onshoring
feed.summary: Rockwell Automation stands to benefit significantly from U.S. tariffs and the growing trend of onshoring manufacturing back to the United States. The company, which produces industrial automation equipment and has over 70% of its products "Made in America," is well-positioned to capitalize on increased domestic production spurred by trade policies. Despite conservative guidance, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, anticipating that Rockwell will mitigate tariff impacts and emerge as a net beneficiary.

feed.title: BSX launches new trading and settlement platform
feed.summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX) has introduced a new, fully integrated trading, clearing, and settlement platform developed in partnership with its parent company, Miami International Holdings (MIH). This technological advancement aims to enhance efficiency, expand market capabilities, and reinforce Bermuda's capital market. The platform integrates MIH's proprietary trading systems with clearing and central security depository technology from Vermiculus, offering scalability and a best-in-class user experience.

feed.title: Sonos has reportedly dropped one of its worst ideas
feed.summary: Sonos has reportedly canceled its plans for a streaming video player codenamed "Pinewood," a product that many observers believed was a disastrous idea for the company. This decision comes after a challenging period for Sonos, including a botched flagship app update and the launch of the indifferently received Ace headphones. By dropping the streaming player, Sonos avoids entering a saturated market with a potentially overpriced device and can instead refocus on its core multi-room audio business.

feed.title: Former 2U and Capital One executive launches AI startup to help companies make better sense of data
feed.summary: Harsha Mokkarala, a former executive at 2U and Capital One, has launched E:cue, an AI startup aimed at helping companies better utilize their data. Based in Rockville, the young company has secured backing from the D.C. investment firm Ardent Venture Partners. The article highlights other related startup news in the DMV area, including investments in other local tech companies.

feed.title: FEPI Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:FEPI
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of the REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI), detailing its price performance, key statistics like AUM and dividend yield, and investment strategy. FEPI employs a covered call strategy on top US technology companies, aiming for income generation and limited capital appreciation. The fund's historical performance, asset allocation, and technical analysis indicators are also presented.

feed.title: JUSA ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:JUSA
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA), covering its dividends, returns, and key financial metrics. JUSA is an actively managed ETF focusing on large-cap U.S. companies, utilizing a three-step investment process that includes research, valuation, and stock selection, with consideration for ESG factors. The fund has an AUM of $195.46 million, an expense ratio of 0.12%, and primarily invests in Electronic Technology and Technology Services sectors.

feed.title: Over 3 in 4 Canadian employers continue to struggle to hire talent: Report
feed.summary: A new report by ManpowerGroup indicates that 77% of Canadian employers face significant challenges in hiring skilled talent, a slight improvement from the previous year, but still above the global average. This talent shortage is widespread across various industries, employee numbers, and regions within Canada, with provinces like Quebec and Ontario showing high percentages. Companies are addressing this by increasing wages, upskilling current employees, and targeting new talent pools, while experts suggest further measures like promoting equal opportunities and continuous training.

feed.title: 3 Undervalued Wide-Moat Stocks With Unbreakable Competitive Advantages
feed.summary: This article identifies three undervalued wide-moat stocks: MSCI, CSX Corporation, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM). Each company benefits from strong competitive advantages and high barriers to entry in their respective industries, leading to sustainable profitability. Analysts see significant upside potential for all three, with TSM offering the highest projected growth.

feed.title: Live Nation Loses Bid to Narrow DOJ Ticketmaster Case
feed.summary: A federal judge in New York denied Live Nation Entertainment Inc.'s attempt to narrow the Justice Department’s antitrust case against it, which seeks to break off Ticketmaster. The DOJ and some 30 states allege Live Nation has illegally monopolized the live events industry, controlling a significant portion of concert venues and ticketing. A trial is scheduled for March 2026.

feed.title: Tariff Implications For Investors: Building A Resilient Portfolio Amid Pullbacks
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan's Global Investment Strategy Team discusses the implications of recent tariff announcements on the markets, noting the S&P 500's first 10% correction in two years. They argue that the current market pullback is a valuation adjustment rather than a fundamental economic issue, citing resilient "hard data" and historical market behavior after growth scares. The article advises investors to build resilient portfolios through diversification, income-generating assets like infrastructure, and inflation hedges such as gold.

feed.title: Movano Evie Ring adds new data tracking features
feed.summary: Movano Health has introduced new data visualization features to the Evie Ring app, focusing on women's health metrics and improving user experience. These updates include Apple Health integration, menstrual cycle tracking, and comprehensive 7-day and 30-day graphs for various health indicators. The app also now allows users to log past workouts even if the ring was not worn, enhancing the device's practicality and utility.

feed.title: Sonim’s Expansion & the Future of Rugged Phones
feed.summary: At MWC25, Sonim Technologies unveiled its accelerated growth in the EMEA region and its expanded product line, including two new professional-grade, consumer-rugged smartphones. Simon Rayne highlighted the company's strengthened presence across carriers and retail, and announced an upcoming thermal imaging smartphone developed in partnership with FLIR. Sonim emphasizes the durability, enterprise-grade features, and reliability of its devices for various industries and users seeking robust performance.

feed.title: US Vs. Europe ETFs: Is The Tide Turning?
feed.summary: European equities have notably outperformed U.S. equities since the beginning of 2025, leading to a shift in investor sentiment, with European investors pulling out of U.S. equity ETFs. This divergence is attributed to geopolitical developments, China's economic rebound supporting European exports, and concerns over U.S. mega-cap tech valuations and tariffs. Despite these shifts, both U.S. and European ETF markets are experiencing robust growth, although European ETFs have grown faster due to lower percentages of registered funds historically.

feed.title: Is Wesco International Inc. (WCC) the Best Stock to Buy According to Seth Klarman?
feed.summary: The article discusses Wesco International Inc. (WCC) as a stock held by Seth Klarman's Baupost Group, which has refocused its strategy after a period of underperformance. Klarman's firm generated a 10% return in 2024 and increased its credit investments. Wesco International (WCC) is highlighted as an industrial company benefiting from growth trends, especially in data centers, and delivered strong Q4 2024 results.

feed.title: NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (QQQH)
feed.summary: The NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on analysis of its holdings. MarketBeat calculates this rating and a price target by considering Wall Street analyst ratings for the individual stocks within the ETF's portfolio. The aggregate price target for QQQH is $53.32, with its top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

feed.title: When Facebook Ads Won’t Help Performance Advertisers
feed.summary: This article discusses when Facebook ads are beneficial for performance advertisers and when they may not be. While Facebook offers extensive targeting and can drive traffic, marketers should be aware of diminishing returns due to factors like inappropriate budgets, ad frequency fatigue, rising CPA, and audience demographics. The piece emphasizes the importance of a diversified media mix and exploring alternative advertising channels for sustainable growth.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (SWKS) Investors Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is reminding investors of Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ: SWKS) who suffered losses to contact them regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Skyworks made misleading statements about its business operations and prospects, particularly concerning its relationship with Apple and its ability to capitalize on AI. Investors who purchased Skyworks securities between July 30, 2024, and February 5, 2025, have until May 5, 2025, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Cruise Operators Navigate Customers’ Insatiable Demand for Bandwidth
feed.summary: Cruise ship operators are facing an insatiable demand for bandwidth from passengers, driven by high-resolution smartphone cameras and a cloud-based "always on" culture. While network management tools prioritize premium users, providers like Starlink are seen as crucial for meeting the immense connectivity needs, despite some market competition and a lack of conventional enterprise-grade agreements. The evolving expectations of passengers necessitate continuous retrofitting of vessels and complex network management to ensure a satisfactory user experience, which is paramount for the industry.

feed.title: Where can investors find defensive assets in today’s evolving markets?
feed.summary: This article explores how investors can identify defensive assets in today's changing market environment, characterized by evolving risks like inflation and varied growth scenarios. T. Rowe Price experts discuss the continued role of the U.S. dollar, the renewed attractiveness of cash due to higher yields, and the changing dynamics of long-maturity U.S. Treasuries. They also touch on the potential defensive qualities of commodities, certain equity sectors like healthcare and consumer staples, and the re-evaluation of the "Magnificent Seven" as a defensive asset class.

feed.title: Yum Brands partners with Nvidia to accelerate AI technologies across its global system
feed.summary: Yum Brands has partnered with Nvidia to advance AI technologies across its global restaurant system, leveraging Nvidia's applications to scale its Byte by Yum platform. The collaboration will initially focus on enhancing voice AI ordering in drive-thrus and call centers, implementing computer vision for operational efficiency, and using AI-generated analytics for restaurant intelligence. Pilots are already underway in select Taco Bell and Pizza Hut locations, with a broader rollout planned for 500 restaurants across all Yum Brands in the second quarter.

feed.title: Apple loses German antitrust fight, faces greater scrutiny
feed.summary: Apple lost its challenge in Germany's top civil court against its classification as a significant market power, a designation that grants antitrust regulators more authority to scrutinize its business practices. The Federal Court of Justice upheld the German cartel office's 2023 ruling, placing Apple alongside Google (Alphabet) and Meta as tech giants subject to measures curbing dominance. This decision strengthens the German antitrust watchdog's position, especially concerning Apple's App Store and tracking regulations, though the EU's Digital Markets Act remains the primary standard for Big Tech.

feed.title: Denny’s debuts Nvidia Breakfast Bytes
feed.summary: Denny's has launched a new limited-time menu item called "Nvidia Breakfast Bytes" to honor Nvidia founder Jensen Huang, who reportedly conceived the idea for Nvidia at a Denny's diner booth. The dish features sausages wrapped in pancakes with maple syrup, reflecting Huang's preferred way of eating it. This launch coincides with Yum Brands Inc. also announcing a partnership with Nvidia to integrate AI technologies across its restaurants.

feed.title: S&P 500 Sell-Off: Here's Why This ETF May Be the Smartest Way to Invest in the S&P 500 Right Now
feed.summary: The S&P 500 has experienced a sell-off, prompting investors to consider alternative ways to gain exposure to the index. This article suggests the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) as a smart investment option, highlighting its diversified sector allocation compared to the traditional market-cap weighted S&P 500, which is heavily concentrated in tech. While the standard S&P 500 has outperformed in the last decade, the equal-weight ETF has shown stronger long-term performance since its inception and during market recoveries not driven by specific sectors.

feed.title: JGRO ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns TSX:JGRO
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the JPMorgan US Growth Active ETF Trust Units (JGRO) traded on the Toronto Stock Exchange. It details the ETF's investment strategy, which focuses on long-term capital growth by investing in large-cap US growth equities, and includes information on its asset allocation, top holdings, and dividend history. The report also covers key metrics such as Assets Under Management (AUM), fund flows, and performance data, along with frequently asked questions about the ETF.

feed.title: MSCI World ETFs too volatile for you? Three investment alternatives
feed.summary: This article explores alternatives to MSCI World ETFs for investors concerned about their volatility or heavy US stock concentration. It highlights three specific ETF options: MSCI World ex USA, MSCI World Equal Weighted, and MSCI USA ex Mega Cap Select, detailing their compositions and past performance. The piece also advises investors on maintaining a calm approach during market fluctuations, recommending against impulsive selling or excessive buying.

feed.title: Here Is Why Yield Hungry Investors Should Buy Schwab's SCHD Over The Safe Vanguard VIG
feed.summary: This article compares two popular dividend ETFs, Schwab's SCHD and Vanguard's VIG, for yield-hungry investors. While both offer multi-sector exposure and low expense ratios, SCHD is highlighted as the better choice due to its higher trading volume, more balanced sector diversification, and significantly higher dividend yield. Although VIG focuses on dividend growers, SCHD provides a better overall package for substantial dividend distributions.

feed.title: 4 tech giants look cheap ahead of earnings
feed.summary: Ahead of their earnings reports, four major tech companies—Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Microsoft—are considered undervalued. Analysts see Apple as undervalued despite recent revenue guidance adjustments, Amazon as continuing its impressive growth, Microsoft showing broad-based strength, and Facebook maintaining its competitive advantages in digital advertising despite recent controversies.

feed.title: Trump tally: J&J plans $55 billion investment to expand U.S. manufacturing
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson announced a $55 billion investment over the next four years to expand U.S. manufacturing, research, and technology. This move aligns with President Trump's goal of boosting domestic manufacturing and will include new facilities and expansions to existing sites. J&J's investment is part of a larger trend of major U.S. companies pledging significant spending to expand U.S. operations.

feed.title: How to Buy Berkshire Hathaway Stock
feed.summary: This article explains how to buy Berkshire Hathaway stock, detailing two main methods: using a brokerage account or engaging a financial advisor. It outlines the differences between Class A and Class B shares, providing guidance on how to choose a brokerage and fund an account. The article also offers an overview of Berkshire Hathaway's business model, its diverse holdings, and its financial profile, emphasizing its stability and diversification for investors.

feed.title: Sports bettors have one app in R.I. More may be on the way.
feed.summary: Rhode Island currently offers only one online sports betting app, operated by International Game Technology (IGT), but new legislation aims to expand the market to at least five vendors by mid-2026. This move comes amid user complaints about the current app's limitations and a 24% decline in sports betting revenue for the state from fiscal year 2023 to 2024. While proponents hope for increased revenue, critics express concerns about a potential rise in gambling addiction.

feed.title: Galileo Is Tops Among Digital Debit Issuers, Javelin Says
feed.summary: A Javelin Strategy & Research report ranks Galileo Financial Technologies LLC's Digital First product as the leader among digital debit card issuers, citing its flexibility, security, and scalability. The product allows consumers to use a digital card immediately while a physical card with the same details is mailed. Thales and FIS also earned strong recognition as overall leaders in the digital issuance scorecard.

feed.title: NetEase Games
feed.summary: NetEase Games announced that the mobile version of Once Human is launching on April 23, 2025, with over 30 million pre-registrations. A pre-registration event offers in-game items and a chance to win an iPhone 16 Pro Max. Additionally, new features like Non-Shutdown Servers and Cross-Character Sharing are being introduced for PC players, alongside a new PvE scenario, Lunar Oracle Phase Three, which launched on March 13.

feed.title: SCHG Vs. VUG: Both Are An Equally Great Way To Capture The Tech Rebound
feed.summary: The article discusses how SCHG and VUG are strong ETF options for gaining exposure to the tech sector, especially given the expected growth in AI spending. While both are considered excellent for long-term investors bullish on technology, SCHG has historically shown a slight edge over VUG due to its top holdings. Both ETFs are characterized by low expense ratios and strong dividend growth, making them suitable for aligning portfolios with future tech advancements and higher valuations.

feed.title: J&J boosts investments in U.S. to more than $55 billion amid looming tariff threats
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson announced plans to invest over $55 billion to construct four new manufacturing plants in the U.S. over the next four years, including a site in Wilson, North Carolina. This significant investment, a 25% increase compared to previous years, comes amidst potential threats of drug import duties from the Trump administration, pushing companies to expand domestic manufacturing. The move follows similar announcements from other drugmakers like Eli Lilly and aims to bolster U.S. production, particularly for medicines treating cancer, immune-mediated, and neurological diseases.

feed.title: J&J touts $55B in planned US investments over 4 years, including 3 new manufacturing sites
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson announced plans to invest $55 billion in the U.S. over the next four years, an increase of 25% from its previous four-year spending. This investment includes building three new manufacturing sites, in addition to a previously announced biologics plant in North Carolina, and expanding existing facilities. The move comes amidst a push for increased domestic investment and follows similar announcements from companies like Eli Lilly and Apple.

feed.title: ACV Auctions CEO George Chamoun sells $5.37 million in stock
feed.summary: George Chamoun, CEO of ACV Auctions Inc. (NASDAQ: ACVA), sold 350,000 shares of Class A Common Stock worth approximately $5.37 million across two transactions on March 19 and 20. The sales were conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established in December 2024. Following these sales, Chamoun retains 2,821,193 shares.

feed.title: J&J boosts US investments by 25% over 4 years amid looming tariff threats
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson plans to increase its U.S. investments by 25% to over $55 billion in the next four years, driven by the threat of drug import duties from the Trump administration. This investment includes building four new manufacturing plants, one of which has already broken ground in Wilson, North Carolina. While some investors note that a portion of these commitments were already planned, J&J aims to enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities to mitigate potential 25% tariffs on pharmaceutical imports.

feed.title: News | Johnson & Johnson to spend $55 billion on US manufacturing sites amid tariff threat
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson plans to invest over $55 billion in domestic manufacturing facilities and expansions across the U.S. over the next four years, citing potential tariffs on imported drugs and aiming to boost its U.S. industrial footprint. The initiative begins with a $2 billion biologics plant in Wilson, North Carolina, which will create 420 jobs and focus on medicines for cancer and other diseases. This move mirrors similar domestic investment trends by other pharmaceutical and tech companies, driven partly by past tax cuts and current tariff threats.

feed.title: Worried About the Markets? This Tech ETF Is Up Over 25% This Year and Could Still Go Higher.
feed.summary: The Invesco China Technology ETF (CQQQ) has surged over 25% this year, outperforming the S&P 500 and the broader tech sector, driven by advancements in Chinese AI. Despite recent market downturns and concerns over tech valuations, this ETF offers exposure to undervalued Chinese tech companies with significant growth potential, especially in the AI sector. With a forward price-to-earnings multiple of just 19, it presents a compelling investment opportunity.

feed.title: Digging into Berkshire Hathaway’s portfolio
feed.summary: This article analyzes Berkshire Hathaway's equity portfolio using Morningstar's Global Risk Model, uncovering its concentrated and evolving nature. Despite Buffett's historical aversion to tech, the portfolio is now notably technology-focused, primarily due to its significant Apple holding. The analysis highlights how Berkshire's investment strategy, though idiosyncratic, shows unique characteristics in liquidity, momentum, moat, value-growth, and volatility compared to the S&P 500.

feed.title: HIDIVE Expands Service to Amazon Prime Video in U.K., Canada, Australia, New Zealand
feed.summary: AMC Networks announced that its HIDIVE anime streaming service is now available as an ad-free add-on subscription on Amazon Prime Video Channels in the U.K., Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. The direct-to-consumer app is also available in these territories. Additionally, HIDIVE has expanded its availability in the United States through partnerships with Samsung, LG, and VIZIO, and updated its website and apps in March 2024 with new features.

feed.title: BCX: Examining Inflation Protection Benefits And Recession Risks
feed.summary: The BlackRock Resources & Commodities Strategy Trust (BCX) is a closed-end fund offering commodities exposure and a high income yield, making it attractive for inflation protection. The fund has historically performed well during inflationary periods, outperforming the S&P 500 year-to-date, and trades at a compelling discount to NAV with a significant yield and monthly distributions. While distributions have been fully covered year-to-date, they were not fully covered for the full year 2024.

feed.title: Why Nano Dimension Stock Crashed on Tuesday
feed.summary: Nano Dimension stock dropped 16.5% after a Delaware court ruled in favor of Desktop Metal, forcing Nano Dimension to go through with its acquisition of Desktop Metal. Nano Dimension had attempted to back out of the $183 million deal announced nine months prior, leading to legal action from Desktop Metal. The ruling mandates Nano Dimension to execute a national security agreement with CFIUS and close the purchase by March 31, or extend the deadline.

feed.title: Shopify Takes Steps To Pull In Billions From Passive Funds
feed.summary: Shopify is moving its US-listed shares from the New York Stock Exchange to the Nasdaq Global Select Market, starting March 31. This strategic move could allow the e-commerce company to be included in major stock indexes like the Nasdaq 100, attracting significant investment from passively managed funds. While inclusion can increase buying power, it also ties stock performance more closely to the broader market, making it a "two-way street" according to analysts.

feed.title: PTC Brings AI-Powered PLM to Hannover Messe 2025 with Windchill AI
feed.summary: PTC is set to preview its AI-powered Windchill AI product lifecycle management (PLM) assistant at Hannover Messe 2025, alongside Codebeamer AI and ServiceMax AI. This initiative demonstrates PTC's commitment to advancing agentic AI across the product digital thread, aiming to enhance engineers' efficiency by leveraging product data for informed decision-making and task automation. The preview will include the Document Vault AI agent, designed to extract critical product information from Windchill-stored documents.

feed.title: ADX: Tech Tilt Exposure With Telling Income And Limited Price Volatility (NYSE:ADX)
feed.summary: The Adams Diversified Equity Fund (ADX) offers investors exposure to large-cap and tech stocks with an 8% of NAV annual distribution yield, blending capital appreciation with consistent income. Its diversified portfolio includes major tech holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and NVIDIA, alongside other sectors. Despite consistently trading at a discount-to-NAV, ADX demonstrates a superior 10-year Sharpe Ratio and lower volatility compared to SPY, making it an attractive option for tech and income-focused investors.

feed.title: The Smartest Trillion-Dollar Stock to Buy With $1,000 and Hold Forever
feed.summary: The article identifies Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRK.A, NYSE: BRK.B) as the smartest trillion-dollar stock to buy and hold forever, comparing its diversification and leadership advantage under Warren Buffett to an index fund. It highlights Buffett's track record of outperforming the S&P 500 and stresses that the company is well-positioned for continued success even after his tenure due to its strong culture and chosen successor.

feed.title: A New Chapter Awaits – Take Centre Stage in F1® 25; An Epic Formula 1® Blockbuster
feed.summary: Electronic Arts announced the release of F1® 25, an official game of the 2025 FIA Formula One World Championship™, coming to PlayStation®5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC on May 30. The game features the return of the fan-favorite story mode "Braking Point," an overhauled "My Team" mode, and increased circuit authenticity through LIDAR technology. Lewis Hamilton is confirmed as the cover star for the Iconic Edition, which will also include content inspired by the upcoming Apple Original Films' F1 movie.

feed.title: Gravity Officially Launches Mobile Version of Roguelike Deck-Building Adventure Game 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse' Globally
feed.summary: Gravity has officially launched the mobile version of its roguelike deck-building adventure game, 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse,' globally. The game, which combines text adventure, card deck-building, and roguelike genres, is set in a post-apocalyptic world where players' choices significantly impact the storyline and gameplay. Available for USD 6.99 on Google Play and Apple App Store, it also offers seven DLC packs for expanded content.

feed.title: What is the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV)?
feed.summary: The article discusses the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV), noting its excellent capital returns over the past five years driven by US tech giants, despite a relatively low dividend yield of 1.01% as of February 2025. It emphasizes that while IVV isn't ideal for large investment income, its overall net return and low management fee make it attractive for long-term growth due to its exposure to strong US businesses benefiting from trends like digitalization and AI.

feed.title: AppLovin shares plunge 20% after third short-selling firm slams company's ad technology
feed.summary: AppLovin's shares dropped 20% after Muddy Waters became the third short-selling firm to accuse the company of violating app store terms of service by "impermissibly extracting" user data for targeted ads. Muddy Waters, following Fuzzy Panda Research and Culper Research, claimed AppLovin's ad tactics involve funneling targeted ads without consent and that e-commerce advertisers are abandoning the platform. AppLovin's CEO previously defended the company's technology against similar allegations, stating such claims are attempts to manipulate stock prices for financial gain.

feed.title: Rivian creates new company aimed at small, lightweight vehicles
feed.summary: Rivian has launched a new company called Also, Inc., which will focus on small, lightweight electric vehicles, including a possible e-bike, to address global mobility challenges. Rivian will maintain a minority ownership, and the new venture has secured $105 million in investment from Eclipse Ventures. Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe will chair Also's Board of Directors, which is staffed by a team of experienced professionals from various tech and automotive companies.

feed.title: Asia Markets Mixed, Europe Falls On Auto Tariff Concerns And Gold Hits All-Time High - Global Markets Tod
feed.summary: On Thursday, U.S. markets closed lower due to concerns over President Trump's proposed 25% tariff on imported cars, impacting automakers and suggesting negative Q1 finishes for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq. European shares also declined on Friday amid rising trade war concerns. Gold prices, however, hit a record high due to increased safe-haven demand stemming from the auto tariffs and geopolitical tensions, with investors now anticipating key PCE inflation data to gauge Federal Reserve rate expectations.

feed.title: The Annoying Bug Making Apple Users Very Angry
feed.summary: Apple users are frustrated with a persistent and unclosable notification bug related to the "Reactions" feature in macOS. The notification informs users that Reactions have been turned off by their browser, but its "x" button is non-functional, leaving the annoying message on screen. This issue highlights Apple's oversight in implementing a minor feature notification without a proper dismiss function, causing significant irritation among its user base.

feed.title: Better Comeback Stock: BlackBerry vs. Unity Software
feed.summary: This article compares BlackBerry (BB) and Unity Software (U), two former high-growth tech stocks that have experienced significant declines. It analyzes their past performances, current business strategies, and future outlooks, concluding that Unity Software, despite its challenges, has a better chance of a comeback due to its stabilizing growth and AI-driven advertising platform, whereas BlackBerry's divestments and declining revenue make its future less certain.

feed.title: VUG ETF Holdings List — AMEX:VUG
feed.summary: This article provides a list of the Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) holdings as of February 27, 2026. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet (Class A and C), Meta Platforms, Amazon, Broadcom, Tesla, and Eli Lilly and Company. The content details the weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each of these top companies within the ETF.

feed.title: A few minutes with Jeff Bush, GM executive director of digital vehicle experience
feed.summary: Jeff Bush, GM's executive director of digital vehicle experience, shares insights from his career journey from Silicon Valley startups like Apple and Google to the automotive world. He discusses GM's commitment to electrification, his vision for over-the-air updates improving user experience, and the company's efforts to modernize its software and hardware stack. Bush emphasizes that GM offers engineers exciting challenges in shaping the future of mobility through safe, reliable, and enjoyable products.

feed.title: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq:CXAI) Announces the Appointment of Terry Blanchard as Senior Vice President of Engineering
feed.summary: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq:CXAI) has announced the appointment of Terry Blanchard as Senior Vice President of Engineering. Blanchard, with over 30 years of engineering leadership experience from companies like Apple, WhatsApp, and NVIDIA, will lead CXAI's global engineering organization and drive the development of next-generation AI technologies for workplace experiences. This strategic hire aims to accelerate CXAI's product roadmap and technical strategy, focusing on creating advanced Agentic AI solutions.

feed.title: TOPT ETF Holdings List — AMEX:TOPT
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed list of the holdings for the iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF (AMEX:TOPT) as of March 2, 2026. feed.title: Apple to Invest Billions in U.S. Manufacturing

This initiative includes building a new factory in Houston for server manufacturing, doubling its U.S. Advanced Manufacturing Fund to $10 billion, and establishing a training academy in Detroit. These efforts aim to support American innovation, high-skilled jobs, and advanced technology fields like AI and silicon engineering.

feed.title: Trump’s new tariffs could be devastating for telco supply chains say analysts
feed.summary: New reciprocal tariffs announced by President Trump are expected to significantly raise the price of telecom equipment, impacting supply chains and potentially forcing carriers to increase service prices. Analysts from Dell'Oro, IDC, and neXt Curve agree that the tariffs will lead to higher equipment costs and lower demand, with OEMs like Dell and HPE likely to be hit harder than Ericsson and Nokia due to their manufacturing locations. These tariffs extend beyond China to countries like Vietnam, India, and Malaysia, complicating diversification efforts and potentially affecting consumer prices for devices and telecom services.

feed.title: Looking for Recession-Proof Stocks? Find the Outperformers
feed.summary: While no stocks are truly recession-proof, certain sectors and strategies can help build a more downturn-resistant portfolio. Historically, consumer staples, healthcare, and utilities tend to perform better during economic contractions due to consistent demand. Financial advisors emphasize the importance of diversification across various sectors to prepare for market volatility and maximize gains during recovery periods.

feed.title: Qualcomm seeks AI expansion with possible Alphawave buy
feed.summary: Qualcomm is looking to expand its presence in the rapidly growing AI market by potentially acquiring UK-based semiconductor maker Alphawave IP Group and has already acquired MovianAI, VinAI's generative AI division. These moves aim to diversify Qualcomm's portfolio beyond mobile phone chipsets, whose revenue growth is slowing, and to strengthen its AI infrastructure offerings to compete with rivals like Nvidia, Intel, and AMD. The company faces urgency in diversification due to Apple phasing out Qualcomm modems in iPhones.

feed.title: Stocks Have Worst Day Since 2020 as Trump’s Tariffs Raise Risk of Recession
feed.summary: US and global stock markets experienced their worst day since 2020 due to new tariffs unveiled by President Trump, which are expected to harm economic growth and increase inflation. Technology and energy sectors were hit hardest, while bonds rallied as investors sought safety in government debt amidst rising recession fears. Analysts warn that the aggressive tariffs could push the Federal Reserve towards looser monetary policy but also complicate inflation control, raising the probability of a recession to at least one-third.

feed.title: Ten things to know about the European Union's new product liability directive
feed.summary: The European Union has passed a new product liability directive (PLD) which significantly expands the scope of product liability laws, impacting manufacturers of both consumer products and digital goods like software and AI. The directive introduces changes such as a shift in the burden of proof, extended liability periods, and new categories for recoverable damages, all aimed at modernizing product liability for the digital age and increasing consumer protection. Businesses operating in Europe face increased litigation risks and costs, with member states obligated to implement these changes into national law by December 2026.

While VIG is a popular dividend ETF with over $102 billion in assets, its dividend yield is relatively low at 1.8%, and its five-year total return of 81% has lagged behind broader market indices like SPY (103%) and QQQ (117%). The technical analysis suggests a possible fall to the year-to-date low of $170, unless it breaks above the $190 resistance level.

feed.title: SmartStop Self Storage Expands Digital Capabilities With Mobile App Rollout Across All Locations
feed.summary: SmartStop Self Storage REIT, Inc. has officially launched its mobile app across all 220 locations in the United States and Canada. This proprietary app offers enhanced digital capabilities such as biometric security, easy payments, unit rentals, and gate access, providing a fully integrated and convenient storage experience for its customers. The company emphasizes its commitment to innovation, making self-storage more accessible and user-friendly.

feed.title: The Magnificent Seven receives another ETF focused on covered calls
feed.summary: Roundhill Investments has launched a new exchange-traded fund, the Roundhill Magnificent Seven Covered Call ETF (MAGY), which focuses on covered calls for the mega-cap tech stocks known as the Magnificent Seven. This actively managed ETF is the latest financial product inspired by the strong performance of these prominent technology companies.

feed.title: I think this $300 neural wristband made me lose some neurons
feed.summary: The author reviews the Mudra Link, a neural input wristband designed for controlling devices via hand gestures. While initially hopeful it could assist with cooking and learning, the device proved frustrating due to issues with sensitivity, precision, and the inability to put it in an inactive mode. Ultimately, the author concluded that traditional buttons and swiping remain more reliable for device interaction.

feed.title: IBM pledges $150 billion to boost U.S. tech growth, computer manufacturing
feed.summary: IBM announced a $150 billion investment in the U.S. over the next five years, with over $30 billion specifically allocated to advance American manufacturing of its mainframe and quantum computers. This move aims to accelerate America's leadership in computing and fuel the economy, following a similar push by competitor Nvidia and amidst discussions about reciprocal tariff policies to boost U.S. manufacturing. The announcement also comes after IBM reported better-than-expected first-quarter revenue.

feed.title: Is Occidental Petroleum Corp (OXY) the Best Stock to Buy According to Billionaire Warren Buffett?
feed.summary: This article examines Occidental Petroleum Corp (OXY) as a top holding in Warren Buffett's portfolio, detailing his increased stake and the company's financial performance. It discusses Buffett's investment philosophy and OXY's operations, including its recent acquisition of CrownRock and expansion into carbon capture. Despite OXY's strong position with Buffett, the article also presents a counter-argument from Jim Cramer and suggests superior prospects in certain AI stocks.

feed.title: LTE Setting Discovered During Garmin Connect Pairing for Fenix 8, Tactix 8, Fenix E, and Enduro 3 Models
feed.summary: An LTE connectivity option has been found during the troubleshooting pairing process in the Garmin Connect app for upcoming models including the Fenix 8, Tactix 8, Fenix E, and Enduro 3. While this discovery suggests Garmin is exploring LTE for these devices, it doesn't guarantee immediate full functionality, as Garmin has historically limited LTE to safety features. The company may expand capabilities in the future or continue focusing on safety and live tracking.

feed.title: Landsea Homes Launches Sales at Pirate Village in Granbury, Texas
feed.summary: Landsea Homes has announced the launch of sales at Pirate Village in Granbury, Texas, featuring 72 new single-family High Performance Homes. These homes incorporate Apple Home™ environment for smart automation and are located near Lake Granbury and the historic downtown square. A grand opening event is scheduled for Saturday, May 3rd, from 11 AM to 4 PM.

feed.title: Reebok launches smart glasses for athletes with ChatGPT powered voice assistant
feed.summary: Innovative Eyewear Inc. has partnered with Reebok to release a new line of smart glasses, Reebok Smart Eyewear, under the Lucyd Eyewear brand. These glasses are designed for athletes, featuring integrated speakers and microphones, and they connect to smartphones to offer AI assistants like Google's Gemini, Apple's Siri, and a custom ChatGPT model for personalized training plans and workout feedback. The partnership aims to capture a share of the growing smart glasses market, currently dominated by Meta, and the initial lineup is priced at $199.

feed.title: The South African billionaire who became CEO at 73
feed.summary: South African-born billionaire Hilton Schlosberg is set to become the new CEO of Monster Beverage Corporation at 73, effective June 13, 2025. He co-founded the company with Rodney Sacks, and their shares have outperformed major tech giants like Apple, Microsoft, and Google. Monster Beverage has shown strong financial performance, with Q4 2024 net sales increasing by 4.7% to $1.81 billion.

feed.title: Sony mulls spin-off of chip business, says report
feed.summary: Sony Group Corp. is reportedly considering spinning off its semiconductor unit, Sony Semiconductor Solutions Corp., which is a global leader in image sensors. The potential move, which could happen before the end of 2025, would involve distributing the majority of Sony's holding in the semiconductor unit to its shareholders, giving the subsidiary a separate stock listing. This comes as the margin on its image sensor products, used in smartphones, has suffered in recent years despite Sony's success in entertainment products.

feed.title: TSMC breaks ground on third facility in north Phoenix
feed.summary: TSMC Arizona has broken ground on its third semiconductor manufacturing facility in north Phoenix, an expansion expected to create roughly 6,000 jobs and produce advanced 2-nanometer chips by the end of the decade. This development coincides with a visit from U.S. Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick, who lauded it as a significant step in the "golden age of American manufacturing." The company's total investment in the U.S. now exceeds $165 billion, including a substantial $6.6 billion from the CHIPS Act, aiming to generate tens of thousands of jobs and inject over $200 billion into Arizona's economy.

feed.title: Market news: Sony considers spinning off semiconductor business for independent listing as early as this year
feed.summary: Sony Group is reportedly considering spinning off its semiconductor business, Sony Semiconductor Solutions Group, which supplies image sensors to major phone and camera manufacturers. This move aims to streamline operations and unlock asset value, despite the semiconductor segment's recent decline in operating profit margin and growth compared to Sony's gaming and music divisions. A potential spin-off and independent listing could occur as early as this year, with Sony possibly retaining a minority stake.

feed.title: Socket Mobile Introduces World's Smallest, Most Affordable Direct Part Mark (DPM) Barcode Scanner
feed.summary: Socket Mobile, Inc. has launched the DuraScan D764, heralded as the smallest, lightest, and most affordable Direct Part Mark (DPM) barcode scanner on the market, designed for precise traceability across various industries. This new device excels at reading DPM codes on challenging surfaces and also scans standard 1D and 2D barcodes, providing a versatile, mobile, and cost-effective solution for inventory management and asset tracking. The D764 seamlessly integrates with iOS and other Bluetooth-enabled mobile devices through Socket Mobile's CaptureSDK, offering high-performance scanning without the need for cumbersome cables.

feed.title: PayPal Earnings Beat, But Slower Spending And Checkout Use Weigh On Results
feed.summary: PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ: PYPL) reported mixed Q1 2025 results, with adjusted EPS beating estimates but revenue falling short. Slower spending and checkout use, along with competitive pressures and potential tariffs, are weighing on investor sentiment despite the company's reiterated full-year guidance and a fifth consecutive quarter of profitable growth.

feed.title: Corning sees second-quarter core sales above estimates as AI boom aids demand
feed.summary: Corning forecasts second-quarter core sales above Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for its optical connectivity products used in AI infrastructure, specifically noting a 106% year-over-year increase in enterprise business sales for Gen AI. The company, a supplier of Gorilla Glass and optic fiber, also benefits from 5G network expansion and potential investments from partners like Apple. Corning reported robust first-quarter core sales and adjusted earnings per share, both surpassing analyst estimates.

feed.title: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Stock is Beating the Market in 2025
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's stock has significantly outperformed the market in 2025, gaining 17.3% compared to a 6.4% loss for the Vanguard 500 Index. This resilience is attributed to its equity holdings not mirroring broader market losses, its substantial cash reserves, and the diversified nature and relative resilience of its operating businesses. While currently considered modestly overvalued, Berkshire Hathaway can serve as a useful complement to a passive equity portfolio due to its historically low correlation with the overall stock market.

feed.title: Why Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) Stock Is Up 4% Today
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) stock rose 4% after the company raised its annual revenue and profit forecasts for 2025, citing resilient demand driven by the AI boom. This positive reaction comes despite investor concerns about China-related tensions, as management reassured the market about their diversified supply chain and the nature of their software business not being subject to tariffs. Investors appear to be focusing on Cadence's strong fundamentals and critical role in supplying major clients like Nvidia and Apple.

feed.title: S&P 500 on a Winning Streak Since November: VOO vs. SPY ETF
feed.summary: The S&P 500 has been on a winning streak since November, largely due to easing trade tensions and strong corporate earnings. This article compares two popular S&P 500 ETFs, VOO and SPY, highlighting their key differences in expense ratio, asset growth, structure, and tax efficiency. While VOO is recommended for long-term investors due to lower fees and better tax structure, SPY remains popular with traders for its high liquidity.

feed.title: Global Sustainable Funds Outperform Year to Date
feed.summary: Global large-cap sustainable funds have outperformed broader market benchmarks year-to-date, despite a market downturn following a peak in February 2025. This article details the performance of the largest and top-performing sustainable funds, highlighting their investment strategies, ESG ratings, and key holdings. It also touches upon changes in Morningstar's sustainability ratings and the evolving landscape for sustainable investing.

feed.title: BlackRock, Inc. Increases Stake in Rigetti Computing Inc.
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has acquired an additional 3,137,133 shares of Rigetti Computing Inc. for $7.92 per share, increasing its total holdings to over 15 million shares. This move reflects BlackRock's strategic interest in the quantum computing sector despite Rigetti's current financial challenges, and the investment could provide crucial capital and credibility for Rigetti's growth and innovation. While Rigetti's stock has seen a recent gain, it remains down significantly year-to-date, making BlackRock's support potentially instrumental for its long-term success.

feed.title: Apple must halt non-app store sales commissions, judge says
feed.summary: A federal judge has ruled that Apple Inc. violated a 2021 court order by continuing to charge commissions on purchases outside its App Store, referring the company for a possible criminal probe. The judge found Apple "willfully" violated the injunction, intended to open up the App Store to alternative payment options. Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney announced Fortnite would return to the US App Store next week following the ruling.

feed.title: PayPal, Apple Succeed in Scrapping Fintiv’s Patent Claims at CAFC
feed.summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) has affirmed a district court's decision that certain claims of Fintiv, Inc.'s patents for a mobile wallet payment system are invalid as indefinite. Fintiv had sued PayPal for infringement, but the court agreed that the "payment handler terms" in the patent claims lacked sufficient structural disclosure, making them indefinite. Separately, the CAFC also dismissed Fintiv's appeal against Apple concerning a Patent Trial and Appeal Board holding, deeming it moot due to the invalidity ruling.

feed.title: BlackRock, Inc. Reduces Stake in Williams Companies Inc: A Strategic Portfolio Adjustment
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. reduced its stake in Williams Companies Inc (WMB) by 9.68% on March 31, 2025, selling over 10 million shares at $59.76. This strategic adjustment reflects BlackRock's ongoing portfolio management. WMB is currently considered overvalued according to its GF Valuation, trading at $58.57 with a PE ratio of 32.18 against a GF Value of $34.46.

feed.title: LG verifies blue PHOLED tech, bringing the power-efficient, long-lasting "dream OLED" closer to market
feed.summary: LG Display has become the first company to verify the commercialization of blue phosphorescent OLED (PHOLED) panels, a significant step towards more power-efficient and long-lasting OLED displays. By partnering with Universal Display Corp (UDC), LG has developed a dual-stack Tandem structure for blue PHOLED, leading to a 15% power saving compared to conventional OLED TVs. This innovation addresses the inefficiency and shorter lifespan of current blue OLED pixels, which contribute to screen burn-in, and is expected to be showcased at SID Display Week 2025 with applications in IT devices.

feed.title: Scorpio Tankers Inc (STNG): Is This Deeply Undervalued Stock a Hidden Gem?
feed.summary: Scorpio Tankers Inc. (STNG) is presented as a deeply undervalued stock, highlighted by its Intrinsic Value to Price (IV/P) ratio of 2.50. This indicates the stock's implied value is 2.5 times its current price, suggesting a significant margin of safety. Supporting its attractiveness, the company exhibits a high buyback yield of 19.50%, a dividend yield of 4.90%, and an exceptional free cash flow yield of 42.77%, pointing to robust financial health and aggressive shareholder returns in the refined petroleum products shipping sector.

feed.title: Why the Capital One/Discover Deal Is About Much More Than Payments
feed.summary: The Capital One and Discover deal, recently approved by regulators, is poised to reshape not just the payments landscape but also the broader banking industry. Experts anticipate significant impacts on deposit generation, small business finance, and even a potential bid for the Apple Card portfolio. The integration of Discover's debit network, exempt from Durbin amendment restrictions, is seen as a key asset that could significantly boost Capital One's national digital banking strategy and competitive edge.

feed.title: Apple, Amazon, Block, Take-Two, Dexcom, MicroStrategy, Duolingo, Roku, and More Movers
feed.summary: Stocks continued to rally on Friday, influenced by robust U.S. jobs data and promising signs of impending trade negotiations between the U.S. and China. This positive momentum overshadowed mixed earnings reports from tech giants Apple and Amazon. The article lists several companies, including Block, Take-Two, Dexcom, MicroStrategy, Duolingo, and Roku, as significant movers in the market.

feed.title: LG.Display Announce the First Commercially-Viable "Dream OLED" Blue PHOLED Panels
feed.summary: LG Display has announced the successful commercialization of blue phosphorescent OLED (PHOLED) panels, a significant step towards realizing "dream OLED" displays. This breakthrough, achieved through a hybrid two-stack Tandem OLED structure, allows for 15% lower power consumption while maintaining stability. The technology will be showcased at SID Display Week 2025 and is expected to expand rapidly into IT devices, potentially improving picture quality and energy efficiency in future OLED products.

feed.title: Apple clears Spotify update under new rules allowing purchases within apps
feed.summary: Apple has approved Spotify's U.S. app update, allowing users to access pricing information and make purchases directly within the app, following a U.S. federal judge's order against Apple's in-app commission policy. This change enables Spotify users to see pricing, purchase subscriptions, and use alternative payment methods. Apple, despite disagreeing and planning to appeal, stated it will comply with the court's decision, marking a significant development for developers seeking fairer competition.

feed.title: 2 AI ETFs Switch Places Amid Tariff Tantrum
feed.summary: Amidst rising volatility and a "tariff tantrum" in early 2025, AI stocks experienced a significant sell-off after an initial rally, leading to a shift between two Direxion leveraged/inverse AI ETFs. The Direxion Daily AI and Big Data Bull 2X Shares (AIBU) initially outperformed, but as volatility surged, the Direxion Daily AI and Big Data Bear 2X Shares (AIBD) gained traction. The article suggests that as volatility subsides, traders might consider entry points using these ETFs or related single-stock ETFs for companies like Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet.

feed.title: BlackRock, Inc. Reduces Stake in TTM Technologies Inc.
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has reduced its stake in TTM Technologies Inc. by 2,886,132 shares, a 15.77% decrease, though TTM Technologies still represents only 0.01% of BlackRock's portfolio. The transaction occurred at $20.51 per share, and despite the reduction and TTM Technologies being considered overvalued with a low GF Score, its stock price has increased by 22.04% since the transaction. First Eagle Investment Management, LLC remains TTM Technologies' largest holder, indicating continued investor interest.

feed.title: BlackRock, Inc. Reduces Stake in The Interpublic Group of Compan
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. significantly reduced its stake in The Interpublic Group of Companies Inc by selling over 5.6 million shares at $27.16 each. This transaction, part of BlackRock's portfolio management strategy, decreased its holding in IPG to 10.70% of its total shares in the company. IPG, a global advertising leader with a market cap of $9.38 billion, is considered modestly undervalued according to GF Value and recently entered a merger agreement with Omnicom.

feed.title: 3 Reasons Every Investor Should Own Fidelity’s FTEC ETF
feed.summary: The article advocates for investors to own Fidelity's FTEC ETF due to its diversified exposure to the information technology sector, significant growth potential, and stability derived from its broad diversification. FTEC offers immediate access to major tech firms, boasts 143% returns over five years, and maintains a moderate expense ratio and volatility compared to the S&P 500. Its portfolio of 283 stocks helps mitigate risks associated with individual company performance.

feed.title: This low-profile CEO is the highest-paid in America with a $101 million paycheck that beat out Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple chiefs
feed.summary: Jim Anderson, the CEO of Coherent, has emerged as America's highest-paid CEO with a 2024 compensation package totaling over $101 million, largely due to stock awards. This figure surpassed the earnings of well-known executives like those from Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple. Anderson's low-profile company, Coherent, specializes in network and laser equipment, and his compensation highlights a rising trend in CEO pay driven by significant stock payouts.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Surprised Alphabet (GOOGL) AI “Didn’t Cannibalize Google” — Still “Doesn’t Care for the Stock”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed surprise that Alphabet's (GOOGL) AI product, Gemini, did not negatively impact its core Google search revenues, despite his personal disinterest in the stock. He lauded the resilience of large technology companies, highlighting their scale, financial strength, and ability to adapt to new challenges like AI. Cramer also emphasized the importance of supporting these "super stocks" against legal challenges.

feed.title: Should iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) Be on Your Investing Radar?
feed.summary: The iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV), launched on May 15, 2000, offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Blend segment of the US equity market, with over $573.87 billion in assets and a very low expense ratio of 0.03%. It is heavily allocated to the Information Technology sector, and its top holdings include Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia, accounting for about 33.49% of total assets. IVV has a Zacks ETF Rank of 1 (Strong Buy), making it an attractive option for investors seeking exposure to this market segment, and is comparable to SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO).

feed.title: PENN ENTERTAINMENT LAUNCHES "PENN Wallet" AT M RESORT SPA CASINO, POWERED BY EVERI'S DIGITAL CASHCLUB WALLET® TECHNOLOGY
feed.summary: Everi Holdings Inc. announced that its digital CashClub Wallet® technology is powering Penn Entertainment's "PENN Wallet" at M Resort Spa Casino in Henderson, NV. This launch provides M Resort guests with cashless payment options across the gaming floor, integrating with existing loyalty and payment systems. The "PENN Wallet" represents Everi's first mobile wallet installation in Nevada and is part of a broader agreement to enhance casino resort experiences nationwide.

feed.title: Apple will appeal contempt ruling in Epic Games case over App Store
feed.summary: Apple has filed an appeal against a U.S. judge's ruling that found the company in contempt of an earlier order in its antitrust lawsuit with Epic Games. The ruling mandated Apple to open its App Store to more competition, allowing developers to use third-party payment options and preventing Apple from imposing "scare screens" or a 27% fee on non-App Store purchases. Apple denies violating the court's terms, and the appeal will be heard by the San Francisco-based 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals.

feed.title: Hilton Negotiated Rates For Business & Leisure Travel 2025 Update
feed.summary: This article provides an updated list of Hilton's negotiated corporate rates for 2025, catering to both business and leisure travelers. It highlights that Hilton, like other hospitality companies, offers discounts to employees of its largest accounts, accessible via specific business-specific pages without the need for complex corporate codes. The author advises users to only book eligible rates and review cancellation conditions, noting that some pages may require business email verification.

feed.title: As Investors Brace for Volatility, Look to Dividend ETF FDVV
feed.summary: Given current market uncertainties like sticky inflation, high interest rates, and geopolitical tensions, many investors anticipate continued volatility. Dividend ETFs, such as the Fidelity High Dividend ETF (FDVV), offer a potential solution for stability and income generation. FDVV invests in large- and mid-cap dividend-paying companies and can provide diversification benefits, offering a consistent revenue stream that can help offset market volatility.

feed.title: Will Macroeconomic Shocks Lead To Business Cycle Recession?
feed.summary: James Kostohryz discusses how individual investors can achieve alpha through asset allocation, particularly by avoiding bear markets and re-entering at favorable valuations. He identifies three major shocks facing the U.S. economy: trade war tariffs, fiscal spending cuts, and labor supply shocks due to reduced immigration, all of which could lead to a business cycle recession. Kostohryz advises investors to monitor real-time economic indicators, maintain cash reserves, and consider defensive positions or inverse ETFs to navigate potential market downturns.

feed.title: Clorox CEO sees shoppers ditching bleach for iPhones ahead of tariffs. Wall Street is skeptical
feed.summary: Clorox CEO Linda Rendle is facing skepticism from Wall Street after suggesting that consumers are prioritizing big-ticket items like iPhones and cars over household staples like bleach due to anticipated tariffs. Investors and analysts are questioning this assertion, implying it's an unlikely consumer behavior pattern. The article highlights Clorox's struggle to convince investors about the immediate impact of tariffs on consumer spending habits related to their products.

feed.title: Alphabet shares sink 7% after Apple’s Cue says AI will replace search engines
feed.summary: Alphabet and Apple shares dropped after Apple's services chief, Eddy Cue, suggested AI search engines would eventually replace standard ones like Google. Cue testified in a federal court regarding the Justice Department's lawsuit against Alphabet, stating he expects to integrate OpenAI, Perplexity, and Anthropic into Safari. The comments highlight potential shifts in the lucrative partnership between Apple and Google concerning default search engine agreements.

feed.title: Not just iPhones! Apple steps up India plans as supplier Jabil eyes second facility for AirPods component
feed.summary: Apple supplier Jabil plans to expand AirPods case production in India by opening a second facility in Tamil Nadu, reducing Apple's reliance on China. Jabil is considering its Trichy facility for the expansion, which would strengthen India's role in Apple's manufacturing ecosystem and deepen Apple's investment in the country. This move follows reports of another Apple partner, Foxconn, assembling AirPods in Hyderabad, indicating a growing focus on India for end-to-end Apple manufacturing.

feed.title: Invesco QQQ Hedged Advantage ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Invesco QQQ Hedged Advantage ETF (QQHG), an ETF created in May 2025 by Invesco with $12.87 million in AUM and 220 holdings. The fund focuses on large-cap equity, specifically Nasdaq-100 stocks, and utilizes an options overlay strategy to partially mitigate downside risk. A comprehensive list of its largest holdings, including NVIDIA, Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft, is provided with their estimated values.

feed.title: Invesco QQQ Income Advantage ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Invesco QQQ Income Advantage ETF (QQA), an actively managed fund that launched on July 17, 2024. QQA focuses on large cap equities, primarily mirroring the NASDAQ-100 Index, and employs an options-based income strategy using equity-linked notes. The fund's top holdings include Invesco Premier US Government Money Inst, NVIDIA Corp, Apple Inc, Microsoft Corp, and Micron Technology Inc.

feed.title: Arm tops $1B quarterly sales milestone, but its stock falls on soft guidance
feed.summary: Arm Holdings Plc reported strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing $1 billion in quarterly sales for the first time, with revenue up 34% year-over-year to $1.24 billion. Despite this milestone and record licensing and royalty revenues driven by Armv9 adoption and data center growth, the company's stock fell over 11% in late trading due to soft guidance for the fiscal first quarter of 2026, which came in below analysts' expectations. Uncertainty regarding potential semiconductor tariffs also contributed to the cautious outlook, as Arm declined to offer full-year guidance.

feed.title: VistaShares Target 15 USA Quality Income ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the QUSA (VistaShares Target 15 USA Quality Income ETF), detailing its stock profile, price, dividend, valuation, expenses, holdings, and ESG performance. It includes vitals such as the issuer, expense ratio, inception date, and tracked index, along with comparative data against other ETFs in its category and segment. The document also lists the ETF's top 15 holdings, concentration analysis, performance metrics, geographic and sector exposure, technical analysis, and offers downloadable financial advisor reports and fact sheets.

feed.title: Google's parent company Alphabet shares plunge over 8% amid AI search competition, malware threats
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. shares dropped over 8% following revelations from an antitrust trial, citing increased AI search competition and new malware threats. Apple executive Eddy Cue testified about declining Google search traffic due to AI alternatives and announced future AI choices in Safari. Google also disclosed a new malware strain, LOSTKEYS, linked to a Russian hacking group, and partnered with Elementl Power to build nuclear plants to power AI data centers, addressing the anticipated surge in electricity usage.

feed.title: Alphabet: Testimony From Apple Search Executive Rattles Investors
feed.summary: Apple's Eddy Cue testified in the DOJ lawsuit against Alphabet, indicating Apple may end its long-term Google Search partnership for Safari. This news caused investor concern, given Google's significant revenue-sharing agreement with Apple. Despite potential changes and AI's impact on search, Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate for Alphabet, believing the market's reaction is overblown and that Google will adapt with AI-infused offerings.

feed.title: Top Chief Technology Officers to Watch in 2025: Maximus’ Mike Raker
feed.summary: Mike Raker, CTO of Maximus, is highlighted as a top CTO to watch in 2025 for his leadership in AI and customer experience initiatives. Raker has focused on implementing AI solutions for government agencies, such as accelerating application processing for the Department of Veterans Affairs, leading to significant efficiency gains. He is also looking ahead to the "next generation of CX" for federal agencies, emphasizing data utilization and advanced analytics to improve public services.

feed.title: Does Apple owe you money? Time's running out to file claim in $95M Siri settlement
feed.summary: Apple has a $95 million class action settlement for a lawsuit alleging its Siri virtual assistant surreptitiously recorded private conversations. Consumers who owned Siri-enabled devices between September 17, 2014, and December 31, 2024, and experienced unintended Siri activations, may be eligible to claim up to $20 per device. The deadline to file a claim is July 2, and a final approval hearing for the settlement is scheduled for August 1.

feed.title: Costco class action alleges iPhones sold there lack required repair, warranty info
feed.summary: A new class action lawsuit has been filed against Costco Wholesale Corp., alleging that the company failed to provide required repair and warranty information on the packaging of iPhones sold in its stores. The plaintiff, Bhaskar Gokarn Mishra, claims Costco's omissions violate Washington's Consumer Protection Act and Telephone Buyers' Protection Act, causing consumers financial harm and preventing informed purchasing decisions. Mishra is seeking to represent a class of consumers who bought iPhones from Costco in Washington and is demanding a jury trial, injunctive relief, and damages.

feed.title: With 1 Update, Delta Air Lines May Have Just Solved the Most Annoying Thing About Travel
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has rolled out a significant update to its Fly Delta App, introducing Live Activities for iOS users. This feature, which activates three hours before a flight, provides real-time updates on travel milestones directly on the lock screen or Dynamic Island, aiming to reduce the stress of tracking flight information during travel disruptions. The update is especially timely as summer travel season approaches, promising a more streamlined experience for passengers.

feed.title: Globalstar: Deploying 17 replacement satellites takes two launches; only one has been booked for this year
feed.summary: Globalstar Inc. announced that it still anticipates receiving all 17 replacement satellites ordered from MDA Space in 2022. However, only one of the two necessary launch slots has been booked so far. The $329.3 million contract with MDA Space includes Rocket Lab as a major subcontractor for the satellite platforms.

feed.title: EXEC: Peloton CEO Highlights Four “Objectives” to Drive Growth
feed.summary: Peloton's CEO, Peter Stern, outlined four key objectives to drive growth after the company raised its full-year guidance for 2025. These objectives include enhancing member outcomes through improved products and services, expanding reach to new members and locations, fostering deeper connections among existing members, and streamlining operations for greater efficiency and revenue maximization. The company reported positive Adjusted EBITDA for the fifth consecutive quarter and raised its full-year guidance for various metrics, signaling a strong financial recovery.

feed.title: Jabil to expand AirPod casing production in India with new plant
feed.summary: Apple supplier Jabil plans to build a second factory in Tiruchirappalli (Trichy), Tamil Nadu, to expand AirPod casing manufacturing in India. This move is part of Apple's broader strategy to diversify its manufacturing operations away from China. Jabil currently produces plastic enclosures for AirPods in its Pune facility and aims to leverage the proposed Trichy site to meet growing demand and strengthen India's role in Apple's supply chain.

feed.title: LDRX Top Holdings List & Exposure (SGI Enhanced Market Leaders ETF)
feed.summary: The SGI Enhanced Market Leaders ETF (LDRX) is an equity fund focused on large-cap strategies within North America, with its top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet, which make up a significant portion of the fund's total assets. The ETF exhibits major exposure to the technology sector, particularly semiconductors and media, and is exclusively exposed to the USD currency and primarily the United States geographically. The article details LDRX's top 25 stock holdings by weight, alongside a comprehensive breakdown of its geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures.

feed.title: IBM Is America’s Worst Tech Company
feed.summary: The article argues that IBM has fallen dramatically from its former prominence as a leading tech company, citing its consistently flat revenue growth and significantly smaller market capitalization compared to competitors like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. It highlights IBM's failure to succeed in key tech sectors such as cloud computing and AI, where its market share is negligible. The author concludes that IBM, despite attempts by its CEO to project AI success, is now among America's worst tech companies, especially when compared to its more successful peers.

feed.title: Product roundup: Franklin Templeton introduces evergreen private equity fund
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton Canada has launched an evergreen private equity fund, the Franklin Lexington PE Secondaries Fund, targeting Canadian accredited investors. This fund aims to capitalize on market opportunities by acquiring private equity investments at discounts in the secondary market. Additionally, the article covers new product launches and fund changes from other firms such as TD Asset Management, Harvest Portfolios Group, Brompton Funds, AGF Investments, National Bank Investments, and Venator Capital Management, including infrastructure strategies, leveraged single-stock ETFs, CLO ETFs, and various fee and risk rating adjustments.

feed.title: Innovator Equity Premium Income - Daily PutWrite ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (SPUT)
feed.summary: MarketBeat provides an aggregate rating and price target for the Innovator Equity Premium Income - Daily PutWrite ETF (SPUT). Based on 826 analyst ratings covering 23 companies (24.6% of its portfolio), SPUT has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $27.79.
