The investment is partly supported by $1.5 billion from the CHIPS and Science Act funding and New York State Green CHIPS Program, aiming to strengthen domestic semiconductor supply chains despite former President Trump's past criticisms of the CHIPS Act.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries will invest $16 billion in facilities in New York and Essex Junction VT
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries announced a $16 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities in New York and Essex Junction, VT. This investment responds to the growing demand for next-generation semiconductors driven by artificial intelligence and aims to onshore critical supply chain components. The company is collaborating with major technology firms like Apple, SpaceX, and AMD to support U.S.-made chip production and strengthen supply chain resilience.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries commits $16B to expand New York, Vermont facilities
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries is investing an additional $3 billion to expand its semiconductor manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities in Malta, New York, and Essex Junction, Vermont, bringing its total commitment to $16 billion. This expansion, driven by diversifying supply chains due to potential tariffs and increasing AI demand, focuses on packaging innovation, silicon photonics, and gallium nitride technologies. The company is collaborating with major firms like Apple and General Motors to reshore production in the U.S.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries plans to spend US$16 billion to boost US chip production
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries, the largest US-based provider of made-to-order chips, plans to invest US$16 billion to increase domestic chip production, with US$13 billion allocated for expanding existing facilities in New York and Vermont and US$3 billion for research into advanced packaging and other technologies. This strategic investment is aimed at meeting the increasing demand for chips, driven partly by the artificial intelligence boom, and ensuring supply security for customers like Apple, Qualcomm, and General Motors. The company emphasizes its focus on essential chips and carving out lucrative niches, rather than competing in the most advanced production market.

feed.title: Lunch Wrap: ASX creeps lower as IperionX lands Pentagon payday
feed.summary: The ASX saw a slight dip, easing lower by 0.1% at lunch, despite strength in miners like Fortescue and a significant 10% jump for Lynas due to a rare earths supply squeeze. The major news came from IperionX, whose shares surged 21% after securing a US$99 million contract with the US Department of Defense for titanium fasteners, transitioning the company from R&D to production. Meanwhile, Magellan Financial Group continued to experience outflows, though at a reduced rate compared to the previous month.

feed.title: LG Display Restructures: Repays 1 Tril. Won Loan, Invests 700 Bil. Won in OLED
feed.summary: LG Display is restructuring its finances and advancing its OLED business by repaying a 1 trillion won loan to LG Electronics early and investing 700 billion won in new OLED technology facilities at its Paju factory. This move follows the sale of its Guangzhou LCD factory and aims to improve financial structure and boost small and medium-sized OLED panel production. The company expects significant improvements in its annual interest expense and will continue investing in large OLED technology.

feed.title: 2 Unstoppable ETFs for Growth Investors to Buy and Hold for Years
feed.summary: This article highlights two ETFs, the iShares Exponential Technologies ETF (XT) and Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), as promising long-term investments for growth investors. XT diversifies across global innovation leaders with a modest expense ratio, while QQQ tracks the top 100 non-financial Nasdaq stocks, offering higher concentration in influential tech giants like Microsoft, Nvidia, and Apple. Both ETFs are presented as "buy and forget" options for potentially market-beating returns.

feed.title: MSTY vs. SPYI: Insane Dividend Yields or Tax-Smart Income—Which Wins?
feed.summary: This article compares two dividend ETFs, MSTY and SPYI, for passive income investors. MSTY offers an astounding 94.37% distribution rate but has a higher expense ratio and is tied to MicroStrategy stock, while SPYI provides more diversification, a lower expense ratio, tax advantages, and a 12.5% distribution rate. The choice between them depends on an investor's tolerance for volatility, tax considerations, and preference for ultra-high yield versus diversified, steady income.

feed.title: AAPB Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:AAPB
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of the GraniteShares 2x Long AAPL Daily ETF (AAPB), including its current price, performance metrics, key statistics, and background information. It details that AAPB is a short-term tactical tool designed to deliver 2x the daily price return of Apple stock, emphasizing the need for frequent rebalancing for holders. The article also includes analysis of its holdings, recent news, technical indicators, and answers frequently asked questions about the ETF.

feed.title: XOVR ETF Adds Anduril, Joining SpaceX to Offer Pre-IPO Access
feed.summary: ERShares, through its XOVR ETF, has added Anduril Industries as a new private equity position, offering retail investors access to pre-IPO companies. This move follows previous investments in SpaceX and Klarna, with Anduril becoming the second-largest private holding for XOVR. The ETF aims to provide diversified, liquid access to high-growth public companies and elite pre-IPO firms, leveraging a proprietary research model to identify category-defining companies.

feed.title: Apple, X, and Airbnb among growing number of Big Tech firms exploring crypto adoption
feed.summary: A growing number of Big Tech firms, including Apple, X (formerly Twitter), Airbnb, and Google, are exploring the adoption of stablecoins to reduce transaction costs and optimize cross-border payments. This interest is driven by a perceived shift in the regulatory environment towards blockchain technology and early successes by companies like Google Cloud in integrating stablecoin payments. While discussions are initial for some, the industry sees stablecoins as a significant upgrade to traditional payment systems.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (AAPL): CEO Tim Cook Is The “Best Mover,” Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has praised Apple Inc. CEO Tim Cook as the "best mover" rather than a first mover, highlighting his strategic ability to perfect existing ideas. Cramer acknowledges potential challenges for Apple, including concerns about its AI development relative to competitors and the implications of a legal battle with Epic Games. Despite these issues, Cramer views AAPL as a favorable investment and lists it as 3rd among stocks he discusses, though he suggests some AI stocks might offer greater short-term returns.
